for you. only ever you.

you were
all of it.

four years of warmth, of laughter, of calls that lasted until the world went quiet — this is for you.

the face i won't forget

you & your flower

all in black, still soft

"some people leave a mark
long before they leave at all."

where it began

It started with a mechanical keyboard.

You replied to a story — something so small, so ordinary — and neither of us knew that reply would become four years of something that felt like nothing else I'd ever known. The universe has a strange and beautiful sense of timing.

There were nights we talked for so long the city outside went completely still. Hours that didn't feel like hours. I don't think I ever told you how much I looked forward to hearing your voice — how it made every ordinary day feel chosen.

this was never a relationship —
it was a friendship that had everything.
goodbye

I don't know how to do this without it feeling like too much. So I'll just say it plainly — it was good. it was really, really good. And I'm grateful I got to know what that felt like, even if just for a while.

I wanted to never stop talking to you. That part was never a question for me. You were the kind of person I never had to explain myself to — and those people are rare. Rarer than I think either of us understood at the time.

I know I pushed too hard at the end. I know the messages were too many. I just didn't know how to let go of something that never felt finished. Still don't, if I'm being honest.

But this is me letting go — not because I want to, but because holding on any longer would only ruin the good parts. And I refuse to let the good parts get ruined.

I hope you're okay. I genuinely, quietly, always hope you're okay.

the boy who meant every word
✦
with love, and then with letting go

this page will always be here.
the internet is permanent — and so is this.
whenever you find yourself wondering if any of it was real,
come back. it was.

a letter, sealed with everything unsaid

✉

there is one last thing
i needed you to read. tap to open

March 2026

Farzana,

I've started this a hundred times. Deleted it. Started again. Because how do you put four years into a page? How do you hold something that big in words that small?

I'm not sure I can. But I'm trying.

You came into my life quietly — the way good things do — and before I knew it, you were just there. In every dumb conversation. Every late night ramble. Every moment I needed someone and didn't know how to ask. You were there without me having to.

I want you to know that I saw you. Not just who you were on the good days — but the version of you that was tired, that was figuring things out, that was trying to hold everything together. I saw that person, and I thought she was the most real thing I'd ever been close to.

I don't have a clean ending for us. No neat bow, no lesson. Just the truth: that knowing you changed something in me that I'm still trying to name. And I think I'll be trying for a long time.

Wherever you go next — I hope it's warm. I hope you laugh loudly. I hope someone makes you feel exactly as seen as you made me feel.

You deserved all of it, Farzana. Every good thing.

Aur hamesha deserved karti rahogi.

yours, always —

Saad

Peace, Amigo ✌️

it was everything. it really was.
